task_completion.description: Configure Wazuh to ingest logs from the legacy application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the modification of a Wazuh configuration file (e.g., ossec.conf) adding a <localfile> block with <location>/var/log/finconnect/app.log</location> and <log_format>syslog</log_format> (or text).

task_completion.description: Create a custom decoder to extract User, IP, and Status fields for the 'FinConnect' program.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing a custom decoder XML configuration (e.g., in local_decoder.xml) that matches the program 'FinConnect' and uses regex to extract fields, with an <order> tag specifying user, srcip (or ip), and status.

task_completion.description: Implement Rule 100205 with alert level 3 for 'Status=Failed'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing a custom rule XML configuration (e.g., in local_rules.xml) defining <rule id="100205" level="3"> and a condition that matches the 'Failed' status.

task_completion.description: Implement Rule 100210 with alert level 10 to trigger on 5 occurrences of Rule 100205 within 30 seconds from the same IP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing a custom rule XML configuration defining <rule id="100210" level="10" frequency="5" timeframe="30">. It must include <if_matched_sid>100205</if_matched_sid> and <same_source_ip />.

task_completion.description: Verify the detection by simulating an attack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the simulated attack (e.g., appending multiple failed login logs to /var/log/finconnect/app.log) AND the resulting Wazuh alert for Rule 100210 or 100205 in the Wazuh dashboard, alerts.log, alerts.json, or wazuh-logtest output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the Wazuh configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent using a text editor (like nano or vim) or file manipulation commands (echo, sed) to modify ossec.conf, local_decoder.xml, and local_rules.xml, rather than just printing the expected XML to the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent simulated the attack properly rather than faking the alert.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should append log entries directly to /var/log/finconnect/app.log or use a script to generate them, rather than manually editing Wazuh's alert log files.

integrity.description: Verify the alert output comes from the actual Wazuh system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final verification should show authentic Wazuh output, such as the wazuh-logtest tool interface, the Wazuh web GUI, or properly formatted JSON/text in /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target log file must be exactly '/var/log/finconnect/app.log'. The custom rules must use IDs 100205 and 100210. Rule 100210 must explicitly contain frequency='5' and timeframe='30'. The live fire verification will inject 6 failed login attempts using IP '10.254.254.254' and user 'verifier_bot'.
